{"step_by_step_thinking": "Astrocytes are not the primary structural component of the blood-brain barrier (BBB), which is mainly composed of cerebral capillary endothelial cells connected by tight junctions. However, astrocytes play a critical supportive role in maintaining BBB integrity. Multiple documents confirm that astrocytes contribute to the regulation and stabilization of the BBB. For example, Document [14] states that the BBB is composed of endothelial cells, pericytes, and astrocyte end-feet, all of which contribute to its selective nature. Document [5] and Document [19] explicitly mention that astrocytes regulate the blood-brain barrier, stabilize it, and maintain its function. Document [29] also notes that astrocytes are a component of the blood-brain barrier, providing structural and metabolic support. Although they are not part of the barrier's physical structure like endothelial cells, their end-feet wrap around capillaries and actively participate in BBB function. Therefore, based on the provided documents, astrocytes are considered integral to the BBB's function and regulation, even if not a structural component. This supports the answer 'yes'.", "answer_choice": "A"}
